specific_mechanism: KSD-Drift: Per-prompt RLHF drift diagnostic using kernelized Stein discrepancy. For each prompt x, sample n=64 completions from current policy π_θ and from frozen reference π_ref. Compute embedding-space score function s_ref(y) from π_ref log-probs. KSD²(π_θ, π_ref | x) = (1/n(n-1)) Σ_{i≠j} u_{ref}(y_i,y_j). Bootstrap-calibrated threshold τ_x flags drift. Returns per-prompt drift score for fine-tuning monitoring.
llm_application: KSD-Drift evaluation-diagnostic: for each evaluation prompt x, sample n=64 generations from the current policy π_θ. Compute the Stein-kernel u_{ref}(y_i, y_j) using (1) RBF kernel on embed(y) and (2) score s_ref(y) = ∂/∂e log π_ref(y|x) evaluated at the embedding of y under the frozen reference. KSD² statistic forms a per-prompt U-statistic. Bootstrap calibration on null draws gives p-value per prompt. Output: drift map over the eval set highlighting prompts where π_θ has departed from π_ref.
novelty_claim: KSD as a per-prompt RLHF drift diagnostic with bootstrap calibration is uncommon. Existing KSD work targets generic goodness-of-fit tests, EBM evaluation, and Stein sampling, not LLM RLHF drift monitoring per prompt.
motivation_justification: KSD is a closed-form proper discrepancy that requires only score function. The candidate transfers the exact U-statistic to LLM evaluation.
sub_mechanisms: M_1: Score function s_ref from frozen reference log-probs
M_2: Stein kernel u_ref on embedding-space pairs
M_3: U-statistic KSD² estimator per prompt
content_words: score function reference frozen log probability
bootstrap null calibration p-value drift threshold
policy reference frozen base LLM comparison

llm_side: policy reference frozen base LLM comparison
source_side: score function reference frozen log probability
